definition: I2S intercepts high-frequency, domain-prominent CMP operands (font-table FOURCCs like GSUB/GPOS/MATH, sfnt header magic, XML/encoding literals, switch-case enum constants, pictographic/mark codepoints) and substitutes them back, over-committing the corpus to the well-known/common structural path and starving the rare, fallthrough, degenerate, or alternative branch that the havoc-only winner reaches by free byte exploration.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes enumerated CURLOPT case-constants back into the selector byte, pinning the option to a known case and starving the default arm; havoc-only naive drifts freely onto non-enumerated values.
signature.one_line: I2S locks selector onto known cases, blocking the default arm

analysis.summary_one_line: An option-selector value matching NONE of the ~250 enumerated CURLOPT_* switch cases, so the default (unknown-option) arm is taken.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The early option-selector bytes (the `option` value switched on at setopt.c:197) must decode to a CURLOPT value that matches NO enumerated case, so control falls through every `case` to the `default:` arm at line 3110.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive (winner) seeds carry varied selector bytes at offset 0x0001 (2f, 01, 06, 05) and hit the default arm 6 times with the W set reaching `default:` exclusively (line 3110 marked [W]); plain havoc byte mutation lands on arbitrary, non-enumerated option values with no force pulling them onto a valid case.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser) seeds are pinned to selector byte 0x01 at offset 0x0001 (all 19 instances), keeping `option` on an enumerated case so they never reach default; I2SRandReplace substitutes the very CURLOPT case-constants the switch compares against back into the input, steering the option toward a matching case and away from the unknown-option default.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The decisive I2S technique HANDICAPS the loser: cmplog's CMP-buffer-driven input-to-state substitution keeps copying enumerated case-constants into the selector, so the option keeps matching a known case. The winner naive has no comparison logging and wins by plain havoc byte mutation that drifts the selector onto an arbitrary, non-enumerated value, taking default. The winner-enabling mechanism is havoc-only.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the default arm of the CURLOPT option switch in Curl_vsetopt.
The switch is dispatched on the option value, so the default arm requires a value matching no enumerated case.
Only the winner-resolving seed set reaches the default arm.
naive is the winner and cmplog the loser, with the technique delta being I2S.
naive resolved the branch in 8/10 trials while cmplog blocked in all 10.
All loser-blocking seeds share selector byte 0x01 at offset 0x0001, while the winner seeds vary there.
A winner-resolving seed carries a non-0x01 selector byte (0x2f) at offset 0x0001.
A loser-blocking seed is pinned to the 0x01 selector byte.
I2SRandReplace substitutes a CMP operand back into the input at matching offsets, which steers the option selector onto enumerated case-constants.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and uses only havoc/token byte mutation, so nothing pulls the selector toward a valid case.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the option selector is read from an early input field would refute this if cmplog reaches the unknown-option default arm at least as often as naive, i.e. if I2S substitution does not bias the selector toward enumerated case-constants.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog's I2S specifically substitutes enumerated CURLOPT case-constants into the selector is inferred from switch structure and the uniform 0x01 loser byte, not directly observed in cmplog CMP-buffer logs.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes font-table literals (sfnt magic, GSUB/GPOS/MATH tags) into leading bytes, locking inputs to well-formed font structure and away from the free byte variation that lands the RTL script case.
signature.one_line: I2S locks inputs to sfnt magic, blocking RTL script case

analysis.summary_one_line: A short/degenerate input whose first 2 bytes route buffer->props.script to an RTL script tag (HB_SCRIPT_THAANA case), reached by byte mutation not CMP substitution.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input whose leading bytes (offset 0x00-0x01) drive buffer->props.script to one of the RTL script tags handled by the switch in hb_script_get_horizontal_direction, taking the true (THAANA-group) case at line 594. The winning inputs are short, low-structure byte blobs (e.g. 2-byte `9b 07`, `97 07`, `b1 07`, `ab 07`) rather than well-formed font files with a valid sfnt header.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds are tiny/loosely-structured blobs found by plain byte havoc mutators (naive/value_profile leaf op ByteInterestingMutator,ByteIncMutator,ByteFlipMutator); their first two bytes vary across many values (offset 0x0000 has 07+ distinct bytes, offset 0x0001 is 07 in 10 of them), exploring the script-tag space until one lands on the RTL case. naive resolves 10/10 and value_profile 8/10.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are well-formed OpenType inputs pinned to the sfnt header `00 01 00 00` at offset 0x00-0x03 with real table tags (GSUB/GPOS/MATH at offset 0x0c-0x0f), so their script never reaches the THAANA RTL case. cmplog/vpc converge on these CMP-satisfying font-structure literals via I2SRandReplace and rarely mutate the leading script bytes freely, blocking 8/10 and 9/10 respectively.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is enabled by plain havoc byte mutation: naive and value_profile share the same havoc+token mutator stack and lack the input-to-state CMP-substitution stage. Input-to-state substitution actively hurts here: cmplog/vpc spend energy substituting CmpLogObserver operands to satisfy font-table magic comparisons, locking inputs into the `00 01 00 00`+tag structure and away from the free leading-byte mutation that lands the RTL script case. This is an anti-roadblock branch where the absence of CMP-driven substitution (havoc-only exploration) is the discriminating capability.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of a switch on the script tag, matching the HB_SCRIPT_THAANA RTL-script case.
The switch dispatches on the script value derived from the buffer/font input.
Both decisive pairs have the non-I2S fuzzer as winner: naive beats cmplog and value_profile beats value_profile_cmplog, both with delta I2S.
value_profile beats value_profile_cmplog with the I2S delta, the second decisive pair.
Winner seeds are tiny, low-structure blobs found by byte mutators; the smallest is a 2-byte naive seed.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=d4ae3009a925c920, size=2 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=2s, mutation_op=ByteInterestingMutator,ByteIncMutator,ByteFlipMutator)

evidence_trail.claim: Loser seeds are well-formed OpenType inputs anchored to the sfnt header 00 01 00 00 with real table tags like GSUB.
At the discriminating leading offsets winners spread across many byte values while losers are pinned to the sfnt header bytes.
At offset 0x01 the loser bytes are uniformly 01 (the sfnt minor-version byte) while winners are 07 across most seeds.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's havoc/token stack, substituting CmpLogObserver operands into the input.
naive and value_profile use only the havoc + token stack with no I2SRandReplace.
The winning side at the blocker switch is taken by inputs absent the GSUB-style font structure (the divergent-branch view shows W takes the true side while L stays false).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 594  T=34 F=338  T=0 F=381  case HB_SCRIPT_THAANA:  <-- BLOCKER

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the THAANA case behind a multi-byte magic comparison (e.g. requiring an exact sfnt header plus a specific script tag) in which cmplog/vpc resolve the branch as fast as or faster than naive/value_profile — showing I2S helps rather than hurts and the winner is not driven by free leading-byte havoc.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal claim that I2S energy-misallocation toward font-structure literals is what blocks cmplog/vpc, rather than a coincidental corpus difference, is inferred from seed structure and not directly observed in cmplog/vpc lineage.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S reads FOURCC tag comparison operands (GSUB, GPOS, MATH) and substitutes them back into the input, steering inputs toward valid well-formed font table tags and away from the degenerate/unrecognized bytes the RUNIC fall-through arm needs.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes FOURCC tags, pulling inputs away from fallthrough

analysis.summary_one_line: A degenerate input yielding an unrecognized/RUNIC script tag so the switch falls through to the HB_DIRECTION_INVALID default arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A short/degenerate byte payload that decodes to an unrecognized script (the RUNIC fall-through arm), NOT a well-formed font with real table FOURCCs like GSUB/GPOS/MATH that route to recognized RTL/LTR script cases.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds are tiny near-random payloads (e.g. 2-byte 'f6 16' and short runs) lacking valid font-table tags, so script resolution falls through the switch to the RUNIC/INVALID arm and the true side at line 660 is taken (W: T=5 F=43 vs L: T=0 F=381).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The I2S fuzzers' blocking seeds are well-formed fonts carrying literal table FOURCCs (GSUB, GPOS, MATH) which steer script resolution onto recognized cases, never reaching the RUNIC fall-through (loser blocked 8/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winning technique is undirected havoc-only byte mutation: the winners (naive, value_profile) lack the input-to-state comparison-substitution stage the losers (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) possess, so havoc is free to produce malformed/unrecognized table tags that decode to the RUNIC fall-through. The losers' comparison-substitution stage reads CMP operands (harfbuzz table-tag FOURCC comparisons) and rewrites input bytes to MATCH those tags, pulling cmplog/vpc onto the valid well-formed-font path and away from the degenerate input that hits the RUNIC arm.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the RUNIC case arm whose true side returns HB_DIRECTION_INVALID, a switch fall-through over unrecognized scripts.
Taking the true side leads to the INVALID direction return.
Winner-resolving seeds are tiny near-random payloads with no font-table tags.

The losers possess the I2SRandReplace mutator that substitutes one CMP operand for the other in the input.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where adding the I2SRandReplace stage to the winner (or removing it from the loser) leaves the RUNIC/INVALID fall-through hit-rate unchanged, showing I2S steering toward valid table FOURCCs is not what diverts the loser away from the branch.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link that I2S substitution specifically pulls cmplog/vpc onto valid-font tags (rather than I2S merely consuming exec budget) is inferred from the FOURCC-rich loser seeds, not directly observed in the loser's lineage.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S intercepts SFNT/OpenType table-tag CMP operands (MATH, GPOS, GSUB) and substitutes them into the header, pinning u.tag to TrueTypeTag and starving the rare DFontTag arm that naive's undirected byte mutation occasionally reaches.
signature.one_line: I2S pins header to SFNT tags, blocking the rare DFont arm

analysis.summary_one_line: Header bytes whose u.tag equals the rare DFont/resource-fork magic rather than the common SFNT/TrueType or OpenType-table tags.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The leading header bytes must form a tag value that the switch on u.tag classifies as DFontTag (Apple resource-fork dfont container), exercising the ResourceForkHeader/ResourceMap path, NOT the TrueType/OpenType SFNT layout. Winner inputs start 00 00 01 00 with the trailing structure-bytes spread across many distinct values; loser inputs start 00 01 00 00 and carry ASCII OpenType table tags (MATH/GPOS/GSUB) keeping u.tag on the TrueTypeTag arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Naive winner seeds begin 00 00 01 00 (offset 0x00-0x03) and resolve the true side at offset 0x01=00, 0x02=01 (W bytes) with no embedded SFNT table tags; the per-role overlay shows only the W set reaching the ResourceFork/ResourceMap functions (e.g. ResourceForkHeader::get_face W=154 L=0) and taking case DFontTag T=154.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds begin 00 01 00 00 (offset 0x01=01, 0x02=00) and embed canonical SFNT/OpenType table tags MATH/GPOS/GSUB/OS at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, so u.tag classifies as TrueTypeTag (L takes case TrueTypeTag T=384) and the OffsetTable/TableRecord::cmp path runs (TableRecord::cmp W=0 L=384) instead of the dfont arm.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is the baseline fuzzer (naive) with only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection; its undirected byte mutation stumbles into the rare DFont magic. Cmplog LOSES because its input-to-state substitution mutator, fed the logged SFNT/OpenType-tag CMP operands, keeps substituting the input toward those well-known table tags (MATH/GPOS/GSUB), pinning u.tag on the common TrueTypeTag arm and starving the DFontTag arm. The enabling technique for the winner is therefore havoc-only mutation.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the DFontTag case-arm of the switch on u.tag inside get_face.
Winner inputs select the DFont arm and exercise the resource-fork path that loser inputs never reach.
Loser inputs instead take the TrueTypeTag arm and run the OpenType TableRecord comparison path that the winner never enters.

evidence_trail.claim: The DFont blocker side is taken by the winner only (T=154 vs L T=0).

evidence_trail.claim: Winner header bytes start 00 00 01 00 while loser header bytes start 00 01 00 00, a tag-classification divergence at offsets 0x01-0x02.
Loser seeds embed canonical OpenType table tags (MATH/GPOS/GSUB) characteristic of the SFNT/TrueType layout.
The winner is the naive baseline fuzzer with no I2S, only havoc and token mutators.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness with a switch on a leading-tag field shows cmplog reaching the rare DFont-equivalent arm at least as often as naive (i.e. cmplog NOT being pinned to the common SFNT/table-tag arm by its CMP operands), the havoc-only-wins / I2S-misdirection mechanism would be refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog's I2S actively misdirects toward TrueTypeTag (rather than merely failing to help) is inferred from loser seeds carrying SFNT table tags, not from a direct lineage trace of an I2S substitution event.

members.function: void hb_ot_map_t::apply<GPOSProxy>(GPOSProxy const&, hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_font_t*, hb_buffer_t*) const

signature.mechanism_summary: Branch has no CMP operand; I2S per-execution overhead and operand-driven substitution divert the loser into expensive GPOS sanitize paths the winner never enters, delaying or preventing arrival at the stage-pause check.
signature.one_line: I2S overhead diverts corpus into GPOS sanitize paths off-branch

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching the GPOS stage-pause check needs a parseable font that drives the shaping loop; no specific literal is gated, so plain havoc reaches it faster than I2S.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font blob structurally valid enough that hb_ot_map_t::apply<GPOSProxy> runs the stage loop and evaluates `if (stage->pause_func)` at line 1989. No specific magic literal or comparison operand gates this branch — it is reached by generic table/loop traversal, not by matching a CMP operand. The branch is therefore an I2S-ANTI branch: presence of I2SRandReplace in the loser fuzzers diverts mutation effort into expensive GPOS subtable sanitize/coverage paths (PairPosFormat1_3, MarkMarkPosFormat1_2, MarkLigPosFormat1_2) that the winners never enter, slowing or preventing the winner's fast havoc-driven arrival at the stage-pause check.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Both winners lack I2SRandReplace (naive and value_profile run only the havoc+token stack). They reach the branch quickly (winner avg duration blocked 0.00h, resolved 10/10) via plain byte/word mutation that builds a parseable font driving the GSUB/GPOS apply loop. The winning seeds carry only havoc/token mutation_op chains (e.g. DwordAddMutator,BytesDeleteMutator,ByteInterestingMutator,TokenInsert...) and contain font-table-shaped bytes (e.g. ASCII tag fragments and 0x20-run filler) sufficient to enter apply<GPOSProxy>.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Both losers (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) add I2SRandReplace / CMP-buffer processing. Their seeds get pulled toward GPOS subtable code: the HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE shows loser-only invocations of GPOSProxy::GPOSProxy (0 vs 189), PairPosFormat1_3::sanitize (0 vs 49) and the Mark*PosFormat1_2 sanitize/get_coverage functions, all W=0 / L>0. This off-chain divergence means the losers spend execution budget where the winners do not, and they block the stage-pause branch (loser avg duration blocked 24.00h/19.20h, resolved 0/10 and 2/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The technique enabling the WINNER is plain havoc/token coverage-guided exploration with no CMP introspection. The branch carries no comparison operand to solve, so the loser's input-to-state substitution stage gives no shortcut here; instead its per-execution cost and operand-driven substitution divert the loser into expensive GPOS sanitize paths, an anti effect. The winner reaches the branch by lucky havoc byte mutation building a parseable font, matching the havoc-only mechanism.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a presence test on a function pointer, not a value comparison against an input-derived operand.
Both decisive pairs have the same I2S technique delta and the winner is the fuzzer that lacks I2S.
The naive>cmplog pair is also an I2S-delta pair won by the non-I2S fuzzer.
The winner reaches and resolves the branch quickly while the loser stays blocked the full campaign.
Losers spend execution budget in GPOS subtable sanitize functions that winners never invoke.
The GPOSProxy constructor is invoked only on the loser side (W=0, L=189), showing the split is off the main chain.
The divergence is off-chain (no divergent branch on the call chain itself).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: (no divergent branches in chain functions; the split is off-chain)

naive runs only the havoc+token stack and has no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

I2SRandReplace works by matching one CMP operand in the input and substituting the other, which requires a comparison to solve.

picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating line-1989 reachability on matching a specific multi-byte literal (a real CMP operand) would let cmplog/vpc resolve at least as fast as naive/value_profile, flipping the divergence direction and refuting the no-operand / I2S-anti hypothesis.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal claim that I2S diversion into GPOS sanitize paths (rather than some unmodeled scheduling difference) is what blocks the losers rests on the HIT-COUNT off-chain signal, which is correlational, not a direct demonstration that I2S caused the diversion.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S substitution steers bytes toward the pictographic comparison operands at line 492, pulling cmplog onto the ZWJ-followed-by-pictographic true branch and starving the false branch; naive's havoc freely produces ZWJ-terminal or non-pictographic buffers.
signature.one_line: I2S steers input to pictographic operands, blocking ZWJ false branch

analysis.summary_one_line: Input containing a ZWJ (U+200D) glyph that is last-in-buffer or followed by a non-Extended_Pictographic codepoint, taking the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A buffer whose decoded codepoint sequence contains a ZWJ (joiner) glyph that is either at the final position (i+1 >= count) or whose following codepoint is NOT an Emoji Extended_Pictographic, so the conjunct at line 491 short-circuits to false.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves the false branch in all 10/10 trials; its winning seeds are dominated by long runs of repeated filler bytes (e.g. 0x85, 0x32, 0x95) and trailing 0d 20 sequences that decode into multi-glyph buffers where a ZWJ appears as the trailing/penultimate glyph, so i+1 reaches count or the next codepoint is not Extended_Pictographic.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the false branch in 9/10 trials (resolved only 1/10); the I2S substitution mutator steers its inputs toward CMP-matched operands so it tends to satisfy the true-branch condition (ZWJ followed by an Extended_Pictographic), keeping i+1<count AND the pictographic test true, which is exactly the blocked-side L behavior shown at line 491 (L true=11, false=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, which has NO comparison introspection — it explores the false branch purely by havoc byte mutation (havoc-only) that happens to produce ZWJ-terminal or ZWJ-then-non-pictographic buffers. The loser cmplog's input-to-state substitution actively biases bytes toward the operands recorded at the line-492 pictographic comparison, pulling it onto the true side and starving the false branch; thus the divergence is attributable to the ABSENCE of comparison steering in naive, i.e. naive's lucky havoc exploration is what enables the winning side.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive>cmplog with delta I2S; naive is the winner and cmplog the loser.
naive resolves the false branch in 10/10 trials while cmplog blocks 9/10.

  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0

The blocker line is a conjunction that first checks i+1<count then the next codepoint's Extended_Pictographic status, reached only inside the ZWJ branch.
The ZWJ branch gates the blocker; only ZWJ glyphs reach line 491.
Loser-blocking inputs take the true branch at line 491 (L true=11, false=0), i.e. ZWJ followed in-range by an Extended_Pictographic codepoint.

naive has no I2S; it mutates purely with the havoc + token stack.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a recorded CMP operand into the input at matching offsets, steering bytes toward the compared value.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the next-codepoint Extended_Pictographic CMP at line 492 is the ONLY gate and cmplog still resolves the false branch as fast as naive — showing I2S steering does not bias cmplog onto the true side and that naive's advantage is not from lacking that steering.

members.function: hb-ot-shaper-hebrew.cc:compose_hebrew(hb_ot_shape_normalize_context_t const*, unsigned int, unsigned int, unsigned int*)

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S over-fits both losers onto Hebrew mark literal switch arms (matching codepoints like 0x05B7/0x05BC), trapping them on the true-branch switch arms and preventing the broad structural/havoc exploration that occasionally yields a successfully-composing base+mark pair.
signature.one_line: I2S traps losers on Hebrew mark switch arms, blocking compose escape

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must drive compose_hebrew to found==true (or set has_gpos_mark) so line 81 takes its false branch instead of the Hebrew fallback.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font/buffer payload where the base Unicode composition c->unicode->compose(a,b,ab) succeeds (found==true) or has_gpos_mark is set, so the conjunction !found && !has_gpos_mark is false. Winning seeds carry BCP47-tag/structural bytes (e.g. 'zh-hant-ht', 'zh-H[nt-ht') that steer the shaper into a font path producing successful composition rather than the Hebrew presentation-form fallback.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds reach line 81 and take BOTH sides (T=14 F=12), i.e. they do reach the false branch and resolve it; avg hitcount on branch winner=8 vs loser=0. They are diverse naive/value_profile havoc/structural inputs (ASCII language-tag runs) that occasionally make found==true so the fallback is skipped.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds hit line 81 only on the true side (T=67 F=0) and never the false side; their bytes are Hebrew-combining-mark-like sequences that miss compose() and keep re-entering the switch(b) fallback. The I2S fuzzers are stuck pumping CMP-matched combining-mark codepoints into the switch arms and never escape to found==true.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The WINNERS are the non-I2S fuzzers (naive beats cmplog; value_profile beats value_profile_cmplog). The escape to the false branch is NOT a CMP-solving event: it needs the broad structural/havoc exploration of the input that occasionally yields a successfully-composing base+mark pair. The I2S/CMP_MAP machinery actively over-fits the loser onto the switch(b) equality arms (matching Hebrew mark literals like 0x05B7/0x05BC), which is exactly the true-branch trap, so the I2S fuzzers blocked. Winner = plain havoc/structural mutation, with no CMP-operand substitution involved (havoc-only).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false branch of the compose_hebrew guard at line 81.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:   if (!found && !c->plan->has_gpos_mark)
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs have the non-I2S fuzzer as winner over the I2S-equipped fuzzer.

Winner seeds carry repeated 4-byte little-endian glyph-record words (e.g. bf 0c 00 00) rather than ASCII sfnt table tags.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The naive winner-resolving seed is filled with structured repeated 4-byte records (bf 0c 00 00 ... bf 0c 00 00) that decode into the glyph sequence needed to set POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH; the W role hits line 850 (the reph-mask loop body) and line 644 (info[start].indic_position() = POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH), both W-only.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser-blocking seeds are dominated by ASCII sfnt table tags surfaced by CMP interception (kern at offset 0x05, GPOS spelled by G/P/O/S at offsets 0x0c-0x0f); these steer the corpus toward valid-table-name inputs on non-reph paths, so info[start].indic_position() never equals POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH and line 849 stays on its false side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, which has no input-to-state mutator and reaches the branch by plain havoc byte mutation (BytesCopyMutator/ByteAddMutator/BytesSetMutator) drifting into the structured-glyph-record byte regime. The delta is the comparison-solving technique: cmplog substitutes intercepted font-table-tag operands (kern/GPOS) back into the input, fixating the cmplog corpus on table-name strings and steering it AWAY from the glyph-record bytes needed for Reph. This is an anti-comparison-solving effect: the enabling technique for the winner is havoc-only byte mutation, and the comparison-solving substitution actively hurts the loser.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes non-ICMPv6 protocol operands captured from other comparisons into the header byte region, steering the corpus away from 0x3a; naive havoc byte mutation lands the ICMP6 proto value by undirected saturation.

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry the ICMP6 next-header byte 0x3a so GetIpProto() selects case kProtoIcmp6.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The parsed IPv6 next-header / protocol field must equal kProtoIcmp6 (0x3a = 58); winner seeds carry a run of 0x3a bytes around offsets 0x07-0x0e so GetIpProto() returns ICMP6 and the switch enters case kProtoIcmp6.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner (naive) seeds hold concentrated 0x3a (ASCII ':') bytes at offsets 0x07 and 0x08 (W bytes '3a(:)x3') and a 0x3a run through 0x0b-0x0d, the literal ICMP6 protocol value, so the true side at L1761 is taken (W: T=3 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser (cmplog) seeds carry 0x00 / 0x29 at offset 0x07 (L bytes '00(.)x7 29())x3') and never plant 0x3a, so GetIpProto() yields a non-ICMP6 value and the switch falls to default (L: T=0 F=32 at L1761, T=32 F=0 at default L1765).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is the I2S-free baseline (naive): its havoc/interesting-value stack lands the 0x3a protocol byte by plain byte mutation, and the I2S-free arm WINS here. cmplog's input-to-state operand substitution, anchored on CMP operands captured upstream, keeps planting non-ICMP6 protocol values and steers the queue toward the UDP/default arms, so the comparison-solving technique is the harmful delta and the surviving feature is reached by plain havoc-only byte mutation.

picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one

signature.one_line: I2S misdirects mutation budget away from the single ICMPv6 proto byte

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode an ICMPv6 next-header byte (0x3a=58) so headers.IsIcmp6() is true; naive's havoc lands it while cmplog's I2S does not.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A parsed IPv6 header whose next-header / IP-proto field equals ICMPv6 (58 = 0x3a). Concretely the input must carry the byte 0x3a in the header region (offsets ~0x07-0x0e in the winner seeds) so that headers.IsIcmp6() returns true at line 131 and GetIcmpHeader().GetType() is reached.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner (naive) seeds are dense with the byte 0x3a in offsets 0x07-0x0e (e.g. '3a 3a 33 31 3a 3a 3a 30'), giving the ICMPv6 proto marker that drives the IsIcmp6() true branch; the byte diff confirms W concentrates on 3a at those offsets while L does not.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser (cmplog) seeds put non-ICMPv6 values in the same header region (offset 0x07 = 00 or 29, offset 0x08 = 7f/40/ff), so IsIcmp6() stays false; line 131 in L is taken false 32 times and true 0 times.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: This is an I2S-ANTI divergence: the winner is plain naive, the loser is cmplog (the comparison-logging fuzzer). naive's undirected havoc/byte mutation stumbles onto the 0x3a ICMPv6 marker by saturating the header bytes with a single repeated value (havoc-only). cmplog adds nothing useful here: the proto check is a constant compare not surfaced as a productive CmpLog operand, so cmplog's extra comparison-substitution stage finds no operand to substitute toward 0x3a and instead spends mutation budget on substitutions that misdirect away from this branch. Plain havoc byte mutation wins; the comparison-substitution technique is a net disadvantage.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of headers.IsIcmp6() at history_tracker.cpp:131, which gates GetIcmpHeader().GetType().
The globally blocked side is the true branch.
The decisive pair is naive > cmplog with delta I2S, i.e. naive is the winner and cmplog (the I2S fuzzer) is the loser.
Winner seeds carry a dense run of 0x3a (the ICMPv6 proto byte, 58) in the header region offsets 0x07-0x0e.
Loser seeds place non-ICMPv6 bytes (00, 29, 7f) in the same header region, so IsIcmp6() stays false.
At line 131 the winner takes the true side while the loser only ever takes the false side (32 times), confirming the IsIcmp6 condition separates W from L.

evidence_trail.claim: The byte-diff signal shows W concentrates on the proto/header bytes that L does not, e.g. offset 0x07 is 3a in W but 00/29 in L.
cmplog's only added technique over naive is I2SRandReplace operating on intercepted CMP operands; naive uses pure havoc/token mutation with no I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on IsIcmp6() in which cmplog reaches the true branch as fast as or faster than naive, showing I2SRandReplace is not a disadvantage here and the divergence is not driven by the 0x3a proto-byte requirement.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog's I2SRandReplace actively misdirects away from the 0x3a marker is inferential; the seeds show naive lands 0x3a but do not directly demonstrate why cmplog fails to.
